message:
runtime: advance local_fsync_offset on replica promotion for WAITAOF parity

When a replica is promoted to master via REPLICAOF NO ONE, any history
that was replicated before the promotion is intrinsically durable from
the old master's perspective — but the new master's local_fsync_offset
still reflected only its own fsync-observed progress, not the inherited
primary_offset. As a result WAITAOF calls issued against the freshly
promoted master would block or time out waiting for a local threshold
that the inherited history had already satisfied.

This commit pins local_fsync_offset to primary_offset at the moment of
promotion, mirroring Redis semantics where the replica treats everything
it has received prior to promotion as locally acknowledged.

Test coverage:
- fr_p2c_006_waitaof_promoted_replica_local_acknowledgment_semantics
  sets primary_offset=100 and local_fsync_offset=10 on a replica,
  promotes it via REPLICAOF NO ONE, then asserts that WAITAOF returns
  [1,0] immediately for a replica count of 1 with zero timeout,
  proving the promotion advanced the local fsync threshold rather
  than waiting on a lagging acknowledgment.

Working tree still contains local-only ephemeral artifacts
(.beads/beads.db.bad_*, .beads/beads.db.pre_rebuild_*,
.beads/recovery_*/, crates/fr-conformance/dump.rdb) which are not
committed — they are backup/recovery snapshots from fixing-beads-problems
runs and a Redis conformance dump, not source-of-truth state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
